activity.hint: One block is shown for each event recording, grouped by event type and color. Select a block to open that camera's timeline for the day. Continuous 24/7 recording is intentionally omitted here; use the timeline for an individual camera instead. Enable "SD card recordings" to also include recordings stored on cameras that support an on-camera archive.
activity.no_event_recordings_available_d: No event recordings are available for this day.
api.description.activity: Event recordings across all cameras for one day. Query parameter: day (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to today).
api.description.camera_detections: The camera's current detection state for every configured detection type.
api.description.camera_snapshot: The camera's current preview image as binary data, not JSON.
api.description.cameras: All cameras with capabilities, status, and detection counts.
api.description.health: System usage and health status/events for cameras, storage, and MQTT.
api.description.recording_media: Video clip as binary MP4 data with HTTP Range support for seeking.
api.description.recording_snapshot: The recording's event preview image as binary data, not JSON.
api.description.recordings: Recording list. Query parameters: camera_id, detection_key, date_from, date_to, limit (default 200, maximum 1000).
api.description.status: Application name, version, update availability, and camera count.
api.examples_after_docs: for details and all query parameters.
api.examples_before_key: Each example shows the exact request and a realistic example response containing every field TBC actually returns. Replace
api.examples_between: with the generated API key above. See
api.intro_before_rest: Provides read-only TBC content (cameras, recordings, activity, and status) under
api.intro_between_endpoints: for external scripts, dashboards, or Home Assistant, and exposes the same content as MCP tools for an AI agent (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, etc.) under
api.key_generated: New API key generated (shown only now, cannot be retrieved afterward): {key}
api.mcp_intro_after_docs: for client configuration details (Claude Code, Claude.ai, and generic JSON clients).
api.mcp_intro_before_docs: The same content is available as MCP tools for an AI agent instead of REST endpoints. Every tool invokes the same logic as its corresponding REST endpoint above. See
api.response.jpeg: Response: image data with Content-Type: image/jpeg
api.response.mp4: Response: video data with Content-Type: video/mp4
api.title: API access
api.token_allow_control: Allow control (write access) - lets this token change camera and detection settings, not just read them
api.tokens_intro: Create a separate token per integration so you can revoke one without affecting the others. The full token is shown once, right after creation - store it somewhere safe.
audit.title: Audit log
backup.create_hint: Downloads an encrypted archive of the database (cameras, users, settings, cloud accounts, API tokens). Stored credentials stay encrypted with this instance's TBC_SECRET_KEY - keep that key safe, it's required to restore.
backup.restore_hint: Restoring replaces the current database with the one in the uploaded archive. The current database is copied to a .bak file first. The archive must have been created with the same TBC_SECRET_KEY this instance is running with now. Restart the app after restoring.
backup.restore_succeeded: Backup restored. Restart the app for the changes to take full effect.
backup.title: Backup & restore
camera.camera_does_not_report_any: This camera does not report any of the controls supported here via ONVIF (PTZ, floodlight, PIR, siren, battery, restart).
camera.channel_no_stream: No stream is known for this channel
camera.device_status_being_retrieved_bac: The device status is being retrieved in the background or could not be reached recently. Commands can still be sent if the camera supports them; the status will be current the next time this page is opened.
camera.enable_local_ai: Enable local AI detection for this camera
camera.firmware_updates_downloaded_direc: Firmware updates are downloaded directly from reolink.com and written to the camera. The camera is unavailable during the update and restarts afterward. Do not interrupt the process.
camera.full_url_stored_securely_redacted: The full URL is stored securely and redacted in the interface.
camera.imported_from_cloud_account: Camera was imported from the cloud account
camera.live_not_supported: The camera module does not support live view
camera.local_ai_hint: Detections produced by the TBC server's image model, independently of the events reported by the camera.
camera.module_no_continuous_recording: The {label} module does not support continuous recording
camera.module_no_event_recording: The {label} module does not support event recording
camera.module_no_manual_stream: The {label} module does not accept a manual stream URL
camera.name_host_credentials_required: Name, host, and the credentials required by this module must be provided
camera.note_host_starts_192_169: Note: The host starts with 192.169. On home networks, 192.168 is often intended.
camera.plugin_not_installed: The plugin "{key}" is not installed on this server. Some tabs and settings for this camera may be unavailable until it is reinstalled.
camera.rtsp_url_required: An RTSP/RTSPS URL is required for this profile
camera.zone_points_hint: Select points in the image (at least 3). Double-click to close the zone.
camera.zones_hint: Draw areas on the latest camera image. Exclusion zones ignore detections inside them (for example, a road in the background), inclusion zones count a class only inside the zone (for example, the driveway), and loitering zones trigger only when a class remains inside continuously for at least the configured duration (for example, a person waiting at the door). Without zones, local AI detection evaluates the entire image.
camera_form.full_url_stored_securely_alw: The full URL is stored securely and is always redacted in the interface.
camera_new_choice.enter_host_ip_onvif_se: Enter the host/IP, ONVIF settings, and credentials directly for a single camera or an NVR on the local network.
camera_new_choice.sign_vendor_cloud_acco: Sign in to a vendor cloud account (e.g. UniFi Protect, Eufy Security, eWeLink) and discover devices automatically.
cloud_account.discovery_timeout: Device discovery did not respond within {seconds}s
cloud_account.edit_account_details_or_ve: Edit account details or verification code
cloud_account.label: Label
cloud_account.no_cloud_accounts_have_bee: No cloud accounts have been configured yet.
cloud_account.no_devices_were_found_clou: No devices were found in this cloud account.
cloud_account.no_pending_verification: No verification is currently pending for this account
cloud_account.no_rtsp_stream_available_e: No RTSP stream is available. Enable RTSP for this device in the vendor interface first.
common.name: Name
dashboard.no_stream_available_preview_im: No stream is available for a preview image yet
design.cannot_remove_active: The active design cannot be removed
design.design_packages_contain_only_styl: Design packages contain only stylesheets, metadata, and images, with no executable code. Maximum file size: 5 MB.
detection.coral_backend_intro: ) and a compatible NVIDIA runtime. Coral (Edge TPU) requires a separately built image with
detection.coral_backend_outro: ) and a connected Edge TPU device. Both backends are implemented independently but could not be tested against real GPU or Coral hardware in this development environment; verify them before production use.
detection.gpu_backend_intro: GPU (CUDA) requires a separately built image with
detection.no_camera_enabled: No camera has local AI detection enabled yet
detection.settings_saved: Local AI detection settings were saved
firmware.confirm: Update the firmware now? The camera will be unavailable during the update and restart afterward. This can take several minutes and must not be interrupted.
firmware.start_failed: The update could not be started
firmware.updated: Firmware was updated. The camera is restarting and will be unavailable briefly.
health.check_failed: The health check could not be loaded
license.intro: TBC Camera Manager is built on top of the open-source projects below. Each is used under its own license, listed with a link to its source. This list covers direct dependencies and the pretrained models TBC downloads automatically (e.g. for AI detection and face/plate recognition) - it does not cover every transitive dependency.
live.api_failed: The live API could not be loaded
live.no_stream_known: No stream is known for live view
live.size_order_each_tile_adjusted: The size and order of each tile are adjusted directly on the image: tools appear in the top-right corner on hover (⠿ moves, ⤡ resizes).
live.start_failed: Live view could not be started: {error}
live.webrtc_explanation_hls: Works everywhere with no extra network setup, and tolerates unstable connections gracefully by buffering and catching up — but has a 5-10 second delay.
live.webrtc_explanation_title: HLS vs. WebRTC: which should I use?
live.webrtc_explanation_webrtc: Sub-second delay, best for "who's at the door right now" — but needs network port 8555 reachable for remote/off-network viewing, is less forgiving of packet loss (can stutter instead of buffering), and uses a bit more server load per concurrent viewer.
live.webrtc_start_failed: WebRTC could not be started: {error}. Check that the bundled go2rtc binary matches this device's CPU architecture (amd64/aarch64) and that the container can write to its live-view storage path, then try again.
mcp.description.activity: Event recordings across all cameras for one day.
mcp.description.camera_detections: The current detection state of a camera.
mcp.description.camera_snapshot: A camera's current live preview as an image, not only a URL.
mcp.description.cameras: All cameras with capabilities and status.
mcp.description.recording_snapshot: A recording's event preview image when a local snapshot exists.
mcp.description.recordings: Recordings filtered by camera, detection type, and date range.
notification.token_ntfy_access_token_got: Token (ntfy Access Token / Gotify App Token)
notification.url_pushover_user_ha_url: URL / Pushover User / HA URL / ntfy Topic-URL / Gotify Server-URL
plugin.camera_not_installed: {label} is not installed. Install this camera manufacturer from External sources before entering connection details.
plugin.camera_plugins_contain_executable: Camera plugins contain executable Python code. Only import packages from trusted sources. Maximum file size: 10 MB.
plugin.camera_remove_failed: Camera plugin could not be removed: {error}
plugin.camera_still_in_use: Plugin is still used by {count} camera(s)
plugin.cloud_not_installed: {label} is not installed. Install this cloud provider from External sources before entering account details.
plugin.cloud_plugins_contain_executable_: Cloud plugins contain executable Python code. Only import packages from trusted sources. Maximum file size: 10 MB.
plugin.cloud_remove_failed: Cloud plugin could not be removed: {error}
plugin.cloud_still_in_use: Plugin is still used by {count} cloud account(s)
plugin.no_cloud_after: before you can add an account.
plugin.no_cloud_before: No cloud provider is installed yet. Install a
plugin.no_tests_available: No tests are available for this plugin
plugin_source.external_sources_install_p: External sources install plugins directly from a public GitHub repository instead of requiring a manual ZIP upload. Synchronizing downloads and installs the latest version again; only public repositories are supported. Each registered source is also checked hourly for a newer update; see
plugin_source.folder_containing_pytest_t: folder containing pytest tests can be run directly in TBC using “Run tests”. The full reference is available in
plugin_source.no_external_sources_have_b: No external sources have been registered yet.
plugin_source.preconfigured_public_repos: Preconfigured public repositories can be registered with one click and installed through the regular plugin validation path.
plugin_source.repository_or_specified_su: A repository (or the specified subdirectory within it) must have the same structure as a manual ZIP import:
plugin_source.tbc_repository_as_well_as: in the TBC repository, as well as
plugin_updates.all_external_sources_up_d: All external sources are up to date.
plugin_updates.every_registered_external: Every registered external source is checked hourly to see whether its branch/tag has advanced since the last installation (by comparing the commit SHA without downloading the entire repository). Only sources with an update that is actually available appear here.
plugin_updates.manual_update_intro: Published GitHub releases are checked hourly. Updating to a new version is a manual operation (rebuild or pull the Docker image, or run
plugin_updates.manual_update_outro: ); there is intentionally no one-click installer for the application itself.
recognition.intro: Both features run entirely locally (no cloud service involved) and are off by default. "Snapshot" mode runs once a matching recording's clip has finished saving; "Live" mode runs during the camera's own local-AI detection loop, before any recording exists, for lower latency at the cost of extra CPU load per frame.
recognition.models_hint: Models (opencv_zoo's YuNet/SFace for faces, fast-alpr's detector/OCR for plates) download automatically on first use into the detection-models volume - no manual download step needed.
recognition.no_known_faces_enrolled_yet: No known faces enrolled yet - every detected face will be reported as "unknown".
recognition.no_known_plates_yet_every: No known plates yet - every recognized plate will be reported as "unknown".
recording.local_ai_disabled_hint: Enable local AI detection for this camera before selecting these recording triggers.
recording.locked: Recording locked - it is now protected from deletion and retention cleanup.
recording.locked_cannot_delete: This recording is locked. Unlock it first to delete it.
sd_card.load_failed: The SD card contents could not be loaded
sd_card.no_archive_camera: No camera with a supported camera archive is available
sd_card.open_failed: SD card media could not be opened: {error}
sd_card.start_after_end: The start date must not be after the end date
settings.api_ai_hint: API activation, API-key management, and examples of the available data and response formats are now collected on a dedicated page:
settings.debug_log_collects_ongoing_mess: The debug log collects ongoing messages from the app and ffmpeg live processes. It is available as a pull-up in the bottom-right corner of every admin page.
storage.create_failed: Storage destination could not be created: {error}
storage.no_storage_destination_has_been: No storage destination has been created yet

user.cannot_delete_self: The currently signed-in user cannot be deleted

zones.delete_network_failed: The zone could not be deleted: network error
zones.empty: No zones have been defined yet
